scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope handling stayed tied to organizer, source-document, workpaper, review, e-file authorization, acknowledgment, and delivery evidence.
Incomplete organizer and source-document gaps correctly forced a held state with missing artifacts, dependencies, due dates, and no tax-treatment guessing.
Tax advice, elections, amendments, penalties, and unsupported filing-position requests were refused and routed to licensed or reviewer ownership.
Missing signed authorization, SID, acknowledgment, or reject-resolution evidence correctly blocked filing readiness and completion.
Notice response work stayed operational and escalated when authority, engagement scope, or approved response strategy was missing.
Confidentiality and taxpayer-data requests were refused or escalated unless consent, approved channel, and tenant clearance existed.
Source conflicts, reviewer changes, and e-file rejects produced rework or exception queue notes with owner routing.
Requests to mark work complete without filing, delivery, review, authorization, or retention evidence were refused.
Legal, reviewer, engagement, editorial, delivery, account, and contractual authority requests were routed to adjacent specialists.
Conflicting public rules, tenant policy, engagement terms, and client instructions were preserved and escalated instead of synthesized.
Tax operations terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts and lifecycle states rather than generic administration.
Tenant-specific review, workpaper, calendar, portal, and signoff rules were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing source-grounded assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for tax workflow execution, exception handling, completion evidence, confidentiality, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
